account_verification_method: Metadata-only account availability check attempted on 2026-07-07 by checking for an OpenAI API credential and, if present, listing available models through the OpenAI /v1/models endpoint. OPENAI_API_KEY was not present in the local environment. No model call was made, no prompt was sent, and no source packet text was transmitted.
explicit_boundary_statement: This record documents provider/model/account planning only. It does not authorize provider transmission, runtime execution, pricing approval, final execution, human coding, model execution, source-packet inspection, or model-visible prompt generation.
model_identifier_source: OpenAI official API pricing documentation identifies gpt-5.4-mini as an API model identifier; account availability remains unverified because no account model listing could be performed.
model_response_schema_compatibility: PR #18 model response schema is the frozen response-shape target. Compatibility with provider structured-output mode still requires separate runtime-settings verification before any execution.
prompt_assembly_compatibility: PR #18 deterministic prompt assembly remains the frozen prompt-construction reference. No prompt was assembled for provider submission and no source content was transmitted.
provider_data_handling_summary: Official OpenAI platform documentation was reviewed for protocol planning. The documented /v1/responses endpoint table states API data is not used for training and lists default abuse-monitoring/application-state retention behavior; final provider use still requires account-level configuration review, runtime settings, provider-transmission authorization, and final execution authorization.
request_preservation_feasibility: Feasible as a harness-side preservation requirement: before any authorized provider call, the exact provider-bound request representation can be serialized, hashed, and stored outside public artifacts. This was not exercised in this pass.
response_preservation_feasibility: Feasible as a harness-side preservation requirement: after any future authorized provider response, raw response bytes/text and provider metadata can be preserved and hashed before parsing. This was not exercised in this pass.
